# SMT-LIB snapshot fixtures

Committed `.smt2` artifacts for the Evident → SMT-LIB path (the
quantifier-free scalar/string subset). Each file is exactly what
`evident dump-smtlib <file> <claim>` writes — a runnable SMT-LIB program
(`declare-const` + `assert` + `(check-sat)` + `(get-model)`).

These are pinned + regenerated by `runtime/tests/smtlib_snapshots.rs`, which
also cross-checks that solving each through the SMT-LIB path
(`Z3_solver_from_string`) yields the SAME sat/unsat as the production C-API
path (`EvidentRuntime::query`).

Regenerate after an intentional emitter change:

```sh
cd runtime
EVIDENT_UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 cargo test --release --test smtlib_snapshots
```

Run any fixture directly through Z3:

```sh
z3 runtime/tests/fixtures/smtlib/scalar_nat_gt.smt2
```

Note: `(get-model)` errors harmlessly under `z3` for the `unsat_*` /
`*_unsat` fixtures — an UNSAT problem has no model, which is faithful.

`ex39_*.smt2` are claims from the real example file
`examples/test_39_string_ops.ev`; the rest are self-contained corpus claims.
